Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 09:50 ET
MACRO READ — Negative Gamma Into Fed

Equity markets face a toxic cocktail of negative dealer gamma (-$13.0B SPY GEX at 0th percentile) heading into today's FOMC decision, with VIX at 23.0 despite CNN Fear & Greed at just 21 — an unusually compressed vol/sentiment relationship that suggests complacency masked by structural amplification risk. The SPY gamma flip at $679.95 sits just $10.74 above current spot, meaning any Fed-driven rally faces immediate mechanical selling from delta-hedging dealers who are structurally short gamma. Flow patterns reveal sophisticated hedging into the event, with massive IWM put blocks (121K calls vs 11.7K puts) creating a small-cap squeeze setup even as broad positioning turns defensive. The 0.27 implied correlation at stock-specific regime levels creates dispersion opportunity, particularly as 19 names trade in vol backwardation (AAPL, AMD, AMZN) while index vol remains suppressed — a structural dislocation that typically precedes single-name breakouts post-catalyst. Quad witching in 2 days amplifies all positioning dynamics, with institutional flow showing stark divergences: Millennium's 398% AMZN reversal and 135% MSFT conviction adds conflict with Druckenmiller's complete META exit and Point72's broad tech reduction. This creates a fragmented institutional landscape where any Fed surprise triggers asymmetric unwinds across the mega-cap complex.

Layer 1: CNN F&G 21Layer 2: SPY GEX -$13.0BLayer 3: IWM call/put imbalanceLayer 4: IC 0.27Layer 9: FOMC + Quad Witch

DESKUS Today's Top News7:07 AM ET
Synopsis (last modified 05:42 ET on 18-Mar-26) S&P futures higher: * S&P futures up 0.5% in Wednesday morning trading following back-to-back gains in US equities to start the week. Most shorted, retail favorites, small caps, travel & leisure, energy and PE among the standouts on Tuesday. Asian markets higher overnight with South Korea up over 5% (regulatory action helped) and Japan up nearly 3%. European markets higher, up ~0.8%. Treasuries extending their recent rally with yields down another 2
Synopsis (last modified 05:42 ET on 18-Mar-26) S&P futures higher: * S&P futures up 0.5% in Wednesday morning trading following back-to-back gains in US equities to start the week. Most shorted, retail favorites, small caps, travel & leisure, energy and PE among the standouts on Tuesday. Asian markets higher overnight with South Korea up over 5% (regulatory action helped) and Japan up nearly 3%. European markets higher, up ~0.8%. Treasuries extending their recent rally with yields down another 2 bp across the curve. Dollar index slightly firmer. Gold down 0.4%. Silver down 0.5%. Bitcoin futures down 0.6%. WTI crude down 1.4%. * No great explanation for the firmer tone in equities today. Stocks seem somewhat less interested in the Middle East, where headlines have been mixed from a risk sentiment perspective. They include continued Iranian attacks on Gulf neighbors, recent Trump comments US would soon be ready to end the war, resumption of some Iraqi oil exports (via pipeline to Turkey) and lack of traction behind US efforts to get more countries to help ease Strait of Hormuz constraints. Positioning dynamics continue to be flagged when it comes to the stabilization/resilience narratives. This week has also delivered some better corporate newsflow, including around AI and airlines. * February PPI out this morning, followed by the March FOMC announcements at 14:00. Fed widely expected to leave rates unchanged 3.5-3.75% and highlight heightened uncertainty in the FOMC statement, while SEP expected to show higher inflation in 2026. Previews noted median dot likely to continue to show one rate cut in both 2026 and 2027. Powell press conference to follow at 14:30. Claims, March Philly Fed manufacturing and January new home sales out on Thursday. No data scheduled for Friday. * NVDA resuming manufacturing of chips for China. FT said MSFT considering legal action against AMZN and OpenAI over their $50B cloud deal. AMZN reportedly planning to sharply cut number of packages it ships through USPS. BABA raising prices for AI computing and storage products amid elevated demand. TCEHY posted fifth straight Q of double-digit growth. LULU Q4 ahead and some positive takeaways surrounding product newness response and full-price demand though core US business still soft and FY26 EPS guidance below on difficult margin setup. DOCU Q4 and FY27 guide ahead with takeaways positive on IAM ramp and $2B buyback announcement. HQY Q4 beat and raised FY27 revenue guidance. TTAM disappointed on 2026 guidance, flagging continued softness in residential and heightened complications from energy price spike. ANDG beat and raised and takeaways positive on first report post IPO. Top Stories (last modified 06:50 ET on 18-Mar-26) Morning Headlines: * Iran conflict: * Trump claims US could end the Iran war soon, also threatens expanded strikes on Kharg Island (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/trump-belittles-allies-that-rejected-his-iran-war-appeals>) * Trump's biggest decision on Iran revolves around whether to retrieve nuclear fuel (NY Times<https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/17/us/politics/trump-iran-nuclear-fuel.html>) * US forces drop multiple 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions on hardened Iranian missile sites, CENTCOM reports (NBC News <https://www.nbcnews.com/world/iran/live-blog/live-updates-iran-war-bunker-busters-israel-beirut-lebanon-hezbollah-rcna264012> ) * Iranian attacks on Israel and Gulf states persist; struck Tel Aviv with cluster munitions (Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/iran-s-attacks-on-israel-arab-states-continue-as-trump-says-us-to-end-war-soon> , Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/china/trump-criticises-allies-over-rejection-hormuz-request-iran-israel-trade-2026-03-17>) * Trump aides predict Iran endgame divergence, noting "Israel doesn't mind the chaos" (Axios <https://www.axios.com/2026/03/18/israel-us-iran-war-objectives-trump-netanyahu> ) * Iran's foreign minister asserts the conflict won't change Iran's nuclear doctrine (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/irans-nuclear-doctrine-not-likely-change-foreign-minister-says-2026-03-18> ) * Israel's targeted operations against Iranian officials escalate regional shadow conflict (link <https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/israel-is-hunting-down-iranian-regime-members-in-their-hideouts-one-by-one/ar-AA1YRKnf> ) * Gulf states urge US to weaken Iran's regime before ending war (link<https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/gulf-states-want-the-us-to-cripple-iranian-regime-before-ending-war/ar-AA1YRiOY>); UAE top adviser says Iran miscalculated attacks (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/uae-official-says-iran-war-tightens-gulf-ties-to-us-israel>) * Energy: * Iraq and Kurdistan agree to resume oil exports via pipeline (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iraqi-government-kurdish-authorities-reach-deal-resume-oil-exports-turkeys-2026-03-17>) * Reopening Hormuz viewed unlikely without a truce (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/iran-war-hormuz-reopening-looks-unlikely-without-a-ceasefire>) * US seizure of Strait could prolong conflict by months (Jerusalem Post<https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/iran-news/article-890309>) * Iran moves crude through the Strait of Hormuz at near pre-war rates, leveraging control amid other exporters' struggles (Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/iran-moves-its-own-oil-through-hormuz-as-it-chokes-other-traffic> ) * Analysts warn of prolonged disruptions, saying Iran remains capable of escalating attacks on Gulf energy (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/69226210-a489-45aa-9993-32ed5359fe18> ) * Emergency oil reserve releases curb crude futures gains as traders adjust positions for future deliveries (Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/trump-s-oil-reserve-release-is-reshaping-the-futures-curve> ) * Trump administration set to announce further easing of sanctions on Venezuela's oil sector (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/us-to-ease-venezuela-sanctions-to-unlock-more-oil-amid-iran-war>) * Japan aims to increase US crude oil imports due to Strait of Hormuz closure (Nikkei <https://asia.nikkei.com/spotlight/iran-tensions/japan-looks-to-boost-us-crude-oil-imports-amid-hormuz-closure> ); urges Iran to ensure safety of ships (Nikkei<https://asia.nikkei.com/spotlight/iran-tensions/japan-urges-iran-to-ensure-safety-of-ships-in-strait-of-hormuz>) * Corporate: * Nvidia secures Beijing's approval for H200 chip sales (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/china/chinese-authorities-approve-nvidias-h200-ai-chip-sales-source-says-2026-03-18>, FT <https://www.ft.com/content/a887194b-fdb3-47ab-89ce-60771a4da43f> ); adapts Groq chip for China (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/china/nvidia-preparing-groq-chips-that-can-be-sold-chinese-market-sources-say-2026-03-17>, The Information <https://www.theinformation.com/briefings/nvidia-readies-new-groq-inference-chips-china> ) * Microsoft considers legal action against Amazon and OpenAI over $50B cloud partnership risks (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/e814f4c3-4fb5-4e2e-90a6-470044436b39> ) * Microsoft revamps AI leadership with Nadella providing direct oversight of Copilot initiative (The Information<https://www.theinformation.com/briefings/microsoft-overhauls-ai-leadership-nadella-take-direct-oversight-copilot>, FT <https://www.ft.com/content/60646cbf-0445-4841-81e1-32efc4b32907> ) * Amazon CEO Jassy predicts AWS sales could reach $600B by 2036, driven by AI demand (The Information <https://www.theinformation.com/briefings/amazon-ceo-jassy-says-aws-reach-600-billion-sales-2036> ) * Amazon aims to cut USPS packages by two-thirds by fall, impacting struggling postal operations (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/business/retail-consumer/amazon-plans-drastic-cut-packages-it-sends-through-post-office-wsj-reports-2026-03-17> ) * OpenAI aims to transform ChatGPT into productivity tool, readies potential IPO by year-end (CNBC <https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/17/openai-preps-for-ipo-in-2026-says-chatgpt-must-be-productivity-tool.html> ) * Anthropic now accounts for over 73% of first-time AI tool spending, according to Ramp data (Axios <https://www.axios.com/2026/03/18/ai-enterprise-revenue-anthropic-openai> ) * Trump administration defends Anthropic blacklisting in US federal court (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/legal/government/trump-administration-defends-anthropic-blacklisting-us-court-2026-03-18>, Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/trump-administration-vows-legal-fight-on-anthropic-ai-tool-ban> ) * Samsung secures preliminary agreement with AMD to develop next-gen AI memory and computing technologies (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/samsung-elec-amd-sign-mou-ai-memory-explore-foundry-partnership-2026-03-18> , Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/amd-ceo-lisa-su-visit-samsung-chip-plant-south-korea-discuss-expanding-ties-2026-03-18>) * Samsung plans to begin volume production of Tesla's chips at its Texas factory in H2'27 (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/business/autos-transportation/samsung-elec-plans-produce-tesla-chips-starting-late-2027-2026-03-18> ) * Samsung considers multi-year deals to alleviate fears of memory chip supply crunch (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/samsung-weighs-multi-year-deals-to-ease-memory-chip-crunch-fears>) * AI start-up Manus, recently acquired by Meta, launches new desktop application for personal laptops (CNBC <https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/metas-manus-launches-desktop-app-to-bring-its-ai-agent-onto-personal-devices.html> ) * Lululemon guides revenue below expectations; tariffs, higher expenses and costs related to proxy fight weigh (CNBC<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/17/lululemon-lulu-earnings-q4-2025.html>) * JPMorgan and other banks halt $5.3B loan deal for Qualtrics with investors baulking at software exposure (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/jpmorgan-halts-qualtrics-5-3-billion-debt-deal-on-software-pain>) * AI drone software maker Swarmer rallies 520% on debut, best IPO performance since Newsmax almost a year ago (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/ai-drone-software-stock-jumps-700-in-best-ipo-since-newsmax>) * BHP names Minerals Americas president and former iron ore boss as CEO (FT<https://www.ft.com/content/ff75ba87-5cd1-4220-b6ae-06cc8f32700c>) * BP to suspend around 800 workers at its Whiting refinery starting 19-Mar due to stalled contract talks (Reuters <https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/markets/bp-locks-out-union-workers-at-its-midwest-oil-refinery/ar-AA1YRIsL> ) * China experiencing OpenClaw frenzy as tech firms rush to create AI agents (FT<https://www.ft.com/content/e242006d-1a8b-403e-9977-74693f7339a9>, NY Times <https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/17/business/china-ai-agent.html> ) * Central banks: * Fed expected to hold Wednesday; policymakers weigh impact of Middle East conflict on both sides of mandate (link <https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/general/powell-s-second-to-last-meeting-previews-an-increasingly-divided-fed/ar-AA1YRgxS> , Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/fed-officials-to-hold-rates-weigh-oil-shock-decision-day-guide>, Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/business/fed-likely-hold-rates-steady-iran-war-shocks-policy-debate-2026-03-18> ) * BOJ seen to hold Thursday; commentary on Middle East conflict may shape April rate hike expectations (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/boj-to-sit-tight-with-oil-surge-set-to-fuel-inflation-day-guide>) * BoE may take aggressive actions against soaring energy prices post-Middle East war, anticipating significant inflation impact (Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/boe-assumptions-suggest-hawkish-tilt-on-uk-rates-economists-say> ) * RBA's split decision on Tuesday's rate hike fuels debate about timing of next move (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/rba-s-rate-hike-split-raises-questions-over-timing-of-tightening>) * Economy: * Economists warn oil price surge will hinder US growth and drive inflation (FT-Booth Survey <https://www.ft.com/content/d4c3f857-eb50-47c5-a109-050a8c2f2346> ) * Global airlines warn of soaring jet fuel prices due to US-Israeli war, predicting higher fares and route cuts (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/uae-temporarily-closes-its-airspace-an-exceptional-precautionary-measure-2026-03-17> ) * Japan manufacturer sentiment rises to highest since December 2021, aided by strong semiconductor sales (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/japan-manufacturers-mood-hits-4-year-high-middle-east-risks-weigh-outlook-2026-03-17>) * Japan export growth slows by less-than-expected, China-bound shipments contract due to LNY holidays (CNBC <https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/japan-february-exports-growth-china-us-shipments-boj-trump-takaichi.html> ) * Japan's annual spring wage talks predict 5% increase for third consecutive year, a 35-year milestone (Nikkei <https://asia.nikkei.com/economy/japan-wage-talks-point-to-5-hike-for-third-straight-year> ) * Markets: * Bond traders scale back Fed rate cut expectations with one 25 bp reduction priced in by year-end (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/traders-dial-back-bets-against-fed-cuts-as-growth-worries-build>) * Global hedge funds endure worst losses since 'Liberation Day' amid Iran war turmoil (CNBC <https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/hedge-funds-suffer-worst-losses-since-liberation-day-on-iran-war-turmoil.html> ) * Barclays market indicator giving strongest buy signal for stocks since Liberation Day tariff fallout in April 2025 (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/barclays-altmann-sees-strongest-buy-signal-for-stocks-in-a-year>) * Some carry trades yield highest returns in three years as traders invest in commodity exporters (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/commodity-currency-carry-trades-see-best-returns-in-years-on-oil>) * Chinese AI stocks tied to OpenClaw surge following Nvidia CEO's positive remarks on AI agents (Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/china-s-openclaw-stocks-rise-as-nvidia-calls-it-the-next-chatgpt> ) * Trade/Tariffs: * Trump anticipates Xi summit in "five or six weeks"; did not announce new date for the summit (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-17/trump-says-he-s-rescheduling-xi-meeting-as-iran-war-rages-on>, link <https://www.msn.com/en-us/politics/government/in-delaying-china-summit-trump-faces-a-familiar-mideast-distraction/ar-AA1YSHV3> ) * Japan PM Takaichi anticipates tough meeting with Trump over Japan's refusal to send ships to the Strait of Hormuz (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/528ea926-606f-4db6-81f1-dc3831b47f6d?syn-25a6b1a6=1> , Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/japan-s-takaichi-visits-trump-as-hormuz-warship-standoff-simmers> ) * Japan and US set to agree on jointly development of rare earths, lithium and copper at upcoming summit (Nikkei <https://asia.nikkei.com/business/materials/japan-us-to-agree-to-jointly-develop-rare-earths-at-summit> ) * Iran conflict transforms shipping market into 'wild west,' with soaring freight rates and scattered container deliveries (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/69f0f8a4-2567-44f4-a7b7-6ce4c62ab0e2> ) Trump says ground troop operations don't worry him, Iranian oil continues to flow through strait: * Trump considering a military operation to seize or destroy Iran's near-bomb-grade nuclear material. Noted operation would be highly complex and risky, that Secretary of State Rubio recently said such an operation would require commando force sent to get it (NY Times<https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/17/us/politics/trump-iran-nuclear-fuel.html>). Trump also told reporters Tuesday that ground operations don't worry him. * The US also bombed Iranian missile sites along Strait of Hormuz Wednesday, with officials saying that the Iranian missile installations posed a risk to international shipping (NBC News<https://www.nbcnews.com/world/iran/live-blog/live-updates-iran-war-bunker-busters-israel-beirut-lebanon-hezbollah-rcna264012/rcrd104756?canonicalCard=true>). * Still not much progress toward offramp, with Axios<https://www.axios.com/2026/03/18/israel-us-iran-war-objectives-trump-netanyahu> citing officials who called Trump most bullish person in the White House on going to war with Iran, appears more aligned with Israeli PM Netanyahu's maximalist objectives than his aides. * However, Trump reiterated Tuesday that the US would be leaving in the near future, though threatened to expand strikes on Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export hub (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/iran-s-attacks-on-israel-arab-states-continue-as-trump-says-us-to-end-war-soon?srnd=homepage-americas>). * Strait of Hormuz traffic freeze somewhat thawing, with reports indicating around 90 ships including oil tankers have crossed the strait since the outset of the war (AP<https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/90-ships-cross-strait-hormuz-050804962.html>). However, Iran still effectively controls traffic, with the country itself exporting more than 16M barrels of oil this month, and is moving crude at similar rates to before the conflict (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-18/iran-moves-its-own-oil-through-hormuz-as-it-chokes-other-traffic>). Other regional agreements also helping loosen crude oil market, with exports from Iraq's Kirkuk fields, Turkey's Ceyhan port have resumed after agreement with Kurdistan Regional Government, with initial capacity of 250K bpd (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iraqi-government-kurdish-authorities-reach-deal-resume-oil-exports-turkeys-2026-03-17/>). StreetAccount Event Preview: March FOMC Meeting * Fed widely expected to leave the funds rate unchanged at 3.5-3.75% at its March 17-18 FOMC meeting this week. However, previews flagged expectations for two to three dovish dissents. Miran and Waller both dissented in favor of a 25 bp rate cut at the January meeting and there have been thoughts that they could be joined by Bowman this time around. * Fed statement not expected to change much in terms of the economic assessment or forward guidance. However, it is expected to include a new mention of the conflict in the Middle East. Previews suggested it could highlight both the heightened uncertainty for the US economic outlook and near-term risks related to upward pressure on inflation and slower economic activity. * The March FOMC meeting will feature an updated SEP. Preview commentary largely focused on the upward revisions to headline and core PCE inflation for 2026 on the back of higher oil prices (and to a lesser extent, firmer core PCE prints). Any other changes to 2026 are expected to be fairly minimal, including the two-sided risk surrounding the growth outlook (AI mentioned as a slight tailwind and oil flagged as slight headwind). * Most previews noted expectations for the 2026 median dot to remain at 3.375%, reflecting one rate cut (it would take three participants coming off their forecast for one rate cut to move the dot higher). While the market was pricing in 60 bp worth of rate cuts for 2026 last month, it is currently pricing in ~25 bp. The 2027 and 2028 median dots are also expected to remain unchanged at 3.125%. Some previews mentioned the possibility of an uptick in the long-run rate. * In terms of Powell's press conference, the market seems most interested in how he frames the risks from the conflict in the Middle East. However, he is expected to harp more on the overall uncertainty dynamic and avoid any policy signaling. When it comes to other factors that could put a dovish/hawkish spin around the press conference, Powell's [...truncated to 2000 words]
OVI Scanner Context
MARKET CONTEXT (recent headlines): - [marketwatch] Fed’s Powell to deliver a gut check for markets as stocks climb alongside oil prices - [marketwatch] It’s a ‘black swan’ moment in oil but nowhere else. The stock market is at risk of a 20% fall, say these strategists. - [marketwatch] Oil prices climb after Iran reports attacks on key oil and natural gas field - [marketwatch] Wholesale prices surge again and show inflation flowing through pipeline of the economy - [marketwatch] Why does everything feel so expensive? Look at wages, not the CPI. - [ft_markets] Oil and gas jump after Iran says energy industry attacked
